title: Getting Started with CDCdata
code: devtools::install_github("https://github.com/patrekieta/CDCdata") library(CDCdata)

title: Getting Started with CDCdata
text: CDC data portal hosts hundreds public health datasets. can search datasets keyword, browse category, explore tag.

title: Getting Started with CDCdata
text: Use cdc_datasets() search datasets: function returns data frame dataset IDs, names, descriptions, categories, tags, row counts. id column contains identifier ’ll use query data.
code: # Search for PLACES data cdc_datasets("PLACES")  # Search for mortality data cdc_datasets("mortality", limit = 10)

title: Getting Started with CDCdata
text: Datasets organized categories. Use cdc_categories() see ’s available:
code: # List all categories cdc_categories()  # Find datasets in a specific category cdc_datasets_by_category("Vaccination")  # Search within a category cdc_datasets_by_category("COVID-19", query = "cases")

title: Getting Started with CDCdata
text: Tags provide granular classification. Use cdc_tags() cdc_datasets_by_tag():
code: # List common tags cdc_tags()  # Find datasets with a specific tag cdc_datasets_by_tag("mortality")  # Exact tag matching cdc_datasets_by_tag("brfss", exact = TRUE)

title: Getting Started with CDCdata
text: ’ve found dataset, use cdc_query() fetch data. function supports SoQL (Socrata Query Language) flexible filtering, sorting, aggregation.

title: Getting Started with CDCdata
code: # Fetch first 100 rows from PLACES dataset cdc_query("swc5-untb", limit = 100)  # Preview a dataset (shortcut for small samples) cdc_preview("swc5-untb")

title: Getting Started with CDCdata
text: Use select parameter return specific columns:
code: cdc_query(   "swc5-untb",   select = c("stateabbr", "locationname", "access2_crudeprev"),   limit = 50 )

title: Getting Started with CDCdata
text: parameter accepts SoQL filter expressions:
code: # Filter by state cdc_query(   "swc5-untb",   where = "stateabbr = 'MN'",   limit = 100 )  # Multiple conditions cdc_query(   "swc5-untb",   where = "stateabbr = 'MN' AND year = '2023'",   limit = 100 )  # Numeric comparisons cdc_query(   "swc5-untb",   where = "access2_crudeprev > 20",   limit = 100 )  # List of values cdc_query(   "swc5-untb",   where = "stateabbr IN ('MN', 'WI', 'IA')",   limit = 100 )  # Text pattern matching cdc_query(   "swc5-untb",   where = "locationname LIKE '%Minneapolis%'",   limit = 100 )

title: Getting Started with CDCdata
text: Use order parameter sort results:
code: # Sort by prevalence (descending) cdc_query(   "swc5-untb",   select = c("stateabbr", "locationname", "access2_crudeprev"),   where = "year = '2023'",   order = "access2_crudeprev DESC",   limit = 20 )

title: Getting Started with CDCdata
text: Combine select group aggregated queries:
code: # Count records by state cdc_query(   "swc5-untb",   select = c("stateabbr", "count(*) as n"),   group = "stateabbr",   order = "n DESC" )  # Average prevalence by state cdc_query(   "swc5-untb",   select = c("stateabbr", "avg(access2_crudeprev) as mean_access"),   where = "year = '2023'",   group = "stateabbr",   order = "mean_access DESC" )

title: Getting Started with CDCdata
text: Use q parameter search across text fields:
code: cdc_query("swc5-untb", q = "diabetes prevention", limit = 50)

title: Getting Started with CDCdata
text: datasets larger 50,000 rows (API limit per request), use cdc_fetch() handles pagination automatically:
code: # Fetch all Minnesota data (with progress indicator) places_mn <- cdc_fetch(   "swc5-untb",   where = "stateabbr = 'MN'" )  # Limit total rows places_sample <- cdc_fetch(   "swc5-untb",   where = "year = '2023'",   max_rows = 5000 )  # Adjust page size for performance places_large <- cdc_fetch(   "swc5-untb",   where = "stateabbr = 'CA'",   page_size = 10000 )

title: Getting Started with CDCdata
text: default, queries return JSON preserves data types. potentially faster downloads large flat datasets, use as_csv = TRUE: Note CSV converts values character strings, may need convert numeric columns retrieval.
code: # JSON (default) - preserves types data_json <- cdc_query("swc5-untb", limit = 1000)  # CSV - may be faster for large datasets data_csv <- cdc_query("swc5-untb", limit = 1000, as_csv = TRUE)  # CSV with fetch data_csv_large <- cdc_fetch(   "swc5-untb",   where = "stateabbr = 'TX'",   as_csv = TRUE )

title: Getting Started with CDCdata
text: Use cdc_metadata() get detailed information dataset:
code: meta <- cdc_metadata("swc5-untb")  # Dataset info meta$name meta$description meta$rows meta$tags  # Column information meta$columns

title: Getting Started with CDCdata
text: Several helper functions make common tasks easier:
code: # Get row count (useful before fetching large datasets) cdc_count("swc5-untb") cdc_count("swc5-untb", where = "stateabbr = 'MN'")  # List column names cdc_columns("swc5-untb")  # Get distinct values for a column cdc_distinct("swc5-untb", "stateabbr") cdc_distinct("swc5-untb", "year")  # Open dataset in browser cdc_browse("swc5-untb")

title: Getting Started with CDCdata
text: Anonymous API requests limited approximately 1,000 requests per hour. higher limits (~40,000/hour), register free app token data.cdc.gov.
code: # Set token for current session cdc_app_token("your-app-token-here")  # Or set in .Renviron for persistence across sessions # CDC_APP_TOKEN=your-app-token-here

title: Getting Started with CDCdata
text: SoQL (Socrata Query Language) similar SQL. ’s quick reference:

title: Getting Started with CDCdata
text: LIKE '%pattern%' - wildcard matching starts_with(column, 'prefix') contains(column, 'substring') upper(column), lower(column)

title: Getting Started with CDCdata
text: count(*), count(column) sum(column), avg(column) min(column), max(column)

title: Getting Started with CDCdata
text: date_trunc_y(date_column) - truncate year date_trunc_ym(date_column) - truncate month

title: Getting Started with CDCdata
text: popular CDC datasets explore: Discover data.cdc.gov use cdc_datasets().

title: Getting Started with CDCdata
text: ’s complete example analyzing health access data:
code: library(cdcdata)  # 1. Find the dataset cdc_datasets("PLACES health")  # 2. Check the metadata meta <- cdc_metadata("swc5-untb") print(meta$columns)  # 3. Check how much data we're dealing with cdc_count("swc5-untb", where = "year = '2023' AND stateabbr = 'MN'")  # 4. Fetch the data mn_health <- cdc_fetch(   "swc5-untb",   select = c("locationname", "access2_crudeprev", "checkup_crudeprev"),   where = "year = '2023' AND stateabbr = 'MN'",   order = "access2_crudeprev DESC" )  # 5. Analyze summary(mn_health$access2_crudeprev)

title: Getting Started with CDCdata
text: independent, open-source project affiliated , endorsed , representative Centers Disease Control Prevention (CDC) U.S. Government. author federal contractor supporting CDC developed package independently. data accessed package publicly available via data.cdc.gov.

title: Authors and Citation
text: Patrick Rekieta. Author, maintainer, copyright holder.

title: Authors and Citation
text: Rekieta P (2026). CDCdata: Access CDC Open Data Portal Datasets. R package version 0.1.0, https://github.com/patrekieta/CDCdata.
code: @Manual{,   title = {CDCdata: Access CDC Open Data Portal Datasets},   author = {Patrick Rekieta},   year = {2026},   note = {R package version 0.1.0},   url = {https://github.com/patrekieta/CDCdata}, }

what: Access CDC Open Data Portal Datasets
title: Access CDC Open Data Portal Datasets
text: CDCdata provides modern R interface CDC’s open data portal (data.cdc.gov), runs Socrata platform. Built httr2, offers: Dataset discovery - Search browse available CDC datasets Flexible querying - Full SoQL (Socrata Query Language) support Automatic pagination - Fetch large datasets progress indicators Rate limiting - Built-throttling respect API limits Authentication - Optional app tokens higher rate limits

title: Access CDC Open Data Portal Datasets
code: devtools::install_github("https://github.com/patrekieta/CDCdata")

title: Access CDC Open Data Portal Datasets
text: Anonymous requests limited ~1,000/hour. Register app token data.cdc.gov increase rate limits.
code: # Set for current session cdc_app_token("your-token-here")

title: Access CDC Open Data Portal Datasets
code: library(CDCdata)  # Set for current session cdc_app_token("your-token-here")  # Search for datasets cdc_datasets("PLACES")  # Preview a dataset cdc_preview("swc5-untb")  # PLACES: Local Data for Better Health  # Query with filters cdc_query(   "swc5-untb",   select = c("stateabbr", "locationname", "data_value"),   where = "stateabbr = 'MN' AND year = '2023'" )  # Fetch large datasets with pagination places_mn <- cdc_fetch(   "swc5-untb",   where = "stateabbr = 'MN'" )

title: Access CDC Open Data Portal Datasets
text: Find data.cdc.gov use cdc_datasets(). Disclaimer: independent, open-source project affiliated , endorsed , representative Centers Disease Control Prevention (CDC) U.S. Government. original author federal contractor supporting CDC developed package independently. data accessed package publicly available via data.cdc.gov.

what: Build a base request to the CDC API — build_request
title: Build a base request to the CDC API — build_request
text: Internal function create httr2 request object common configuration: headers, authentication, retry logic, rate limiting.

title: Build a base request to the CDC API — build_request
code: build_request(dataset_id, base_url = .cdc_base_url, format = "json")

title: Build a base request to the CDC API — build_request
text: dataset_id Character. Dataset ID found data.cdc.gov. base_url Character. base URL (default: data.cdc.gov). format Character. Response format: "json" "csv".

title: Build a base request to the CDC API — build_request
text: httr2 request object.

what: Build a base request to the CDC API — build_request2
title: Build a base request to the CDC API — build_request2
text: Internal function create httr2 request object common configuration: headers, authentication, retry logic, rate limiting.

title: Build a base request to the CDC API — build_request2
code: build_request2(endpoint, base_url = .cdc_base_url, format = "json")

title: Build a base request to the CDC API — build_request2
text: endpoint Character. API endpoint path. base_url Character. base URL (default: data.cdc.gov). format Character. Response format: "json" "csv".

title: Build a base request to the CDC API — build_request2
text: httr2 request object.

what: Set and Get App token from CDC — cdc_app_token
title: Set and Get App token from CDC — cdc_app_token
text: Helper function setting getting CDC App token use throughout package. strictyly necessary data.cdc.gov allows anonymous requests. App tokens useful security reasons bypass rate limits forced anonymous API requests.

title: Set and Get App token from CDC — cdc_app_token
code: cdc_app_token(token = NULL, quiet = FALSE)  cdc_set_token(token)  cdc_get_token()

title: Set and Get App token from CDC — cdc_app_token
text: token App token data.cdc.gov. character string gets attached system environment security. quiet Boolean. Whether include console outputs running function.

title: Set and Get App token from CDC — cdc_app_token
text: Character. app token.

title: Set and Get App token from CDC — cdc_app_token
text: receive app token, can go https://data.cdc.gov/signup. setting account, can go developer settings create new app token.

title: Set and Get App token from CDC — cdc_app_token
code: cdc_app_token() #> ℹ No app token set. Anonymous requests have lower rate limits. #> ℹ Get a token at <https://data.cdc.gov/signup>  cdc_app_token(token = "APP_TOKEN") #> ✔ CDC app token set for this session.  cdc_set_token(token = "APP_TOKEN") #> ✔ CDC app token set for this session.  cdc_get_token() #> [1] "APP_TOKEN"

title: Open Dataset in Browser — cdc_browse
text: Opens CDC data portal page dataset default web browser.

title: Open Dataset in Browser — cdc_browse
code: cdc_browse(dataset_id)

title: Open Dataset in Browser — cdc_browse
text: dataset_id Character. dataset identifier.

title: Open Dataset in Browser — cdc_browse
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ cdc_browse("swc5-untb") } # }

title: List Available Categories — cdc_categories
text: Returns list dataset categories available data.cdc.gov. Use category names cdc_datasets_category().

title: List Available Categories — cdc_categories
code: cdc_categories(limit = 100)

title: List Available Categories — cdc_categories
text: limit Integer. Maximum categories return (default 100).

title: List Available Categories — cdc_categories
text: data.frame columns: category: Category name count: Number datasets category

title: List Available Categories — cdc_categories
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ # List all categories cdc_categories()  # See which categories have the most datasets cdc_categories() |> dplyr::arrange(dplyr::desc(count)) } # }

what: Get Column Names for a Dataset — cdc_columns
title: Get Column Names for a Dataset — cdc_columns
text: Returns column (field) names dataset. Useful building queries.

title: Get Column Names for a Dataset — cdc_columns
code: cdc_columns(dataset_id)

title: Get Column Names for a Dataset — cdc_columns
text: dataset_id Character. dataset identifier.

title: Get Column Names for a Dataset — cdc_columns
text: Character vector column names.

title: Get Column Names for a Dataset — cdc_columns
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ cdc_columns("swc5-untb") } # }

what: Get Row Count for a Dataset — cdc_count
title: Get Row Count for a Dataset — cdc_count
text: Returns number rows dataset (matching filter). Useful checking dataset size fetching.

title: Get Row Count for a Dataset — cdc_count
code: cdc_count(dataset_id, where = NULL, ...)

title: Get Row Count for a Dataset — cdc_count
text: dataset_id Character. unique dataset identifier (e.g., "swc5-untb" PLACES data). can find dataset URL via cdc_datasets(). Character. SoQL clause filtering rows. Example: "year = '2023' stateabbr = 'MN'" ... Arguments passed cdc_query()

title: Get Row Count for a Dataset — cdc_count
text: Integer. row count.

title: Get Row Count for a Dataset — cdc_count
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ cdc_count("swc5-untb")  cdc_count("swc5-untb", where = "stateabbr = 'MN'") } # }

title: Search CDC Datasets — cdc_datasets
text: Searches CDC data portal catalog discover available datasets. Uses Socrata Discovery API find datasets keyword.

title: Search CDC Datasets — cdc_datasets
code: cdc_datasets(query = NULL, category = NULL, limit = 20, only_datasets = TRUE)

title: Search CDC Datasets — cdc_datasets
text: query Character. Search terms find datasets. NULL, returns recent/popular datasets. category Character. Filter category (e.g., "Health Statistics", "Vaccination"). Case-insensitive partial matching. limit Integer. Maximum number results (default 20, max 100). only_datasets Logical. TRUE (default), excludes maps, charts, non-tabular resources.

title: Search CDC Datasets — cdc_datasets
text: data.frame columns: id: Dataset identifier use cdc_query() name: Dataset title description: Brief description category: Dataset category tags: Comma-separated list tags updated_at: Last update timestamp rows: Approximate row count (available) url: Direct link dataset page

title: Search CDC Datasets — cdc_datasets
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ cdc_datasets("PLACES")  cdc_datasets("mortality", limit = 10)  cdc_datasets("vaccination", category = "COVID-19") } # }

title: Search Datasets by Category — cdc_datasets_category
text: Finds datasets within specific category data.cdc.gov. Use cdc_categories() see available categories.

title: Search Datasets by Category — cdc_datasets_category
code: cdc_datasets_category(category, query = NULL, limit = 20, exact = FALSE)

title: Search Datasets by Category — cdc_datasets_category
text: category Character. category name search . Supports partial matching (case-insensitive). query Character. Optional additional search terms filter within category. limit Integer. Maximum number results (default 20, max 100). exact Logical. TRUE, requires exact category match. FALSE (default), uses partial matching.

title: Search Datasets by Category — cdc_datasets_category
text: data.frame columns: id: Dataset identifier use cdc_query() name: Dataset title description: Brief description category: Dataset category tags: Comma-separated list tags updated_at: Last update timestamp rows: Approximate row count (available) url: Direct link dataset page

title: Search Datasets by Category — cdc_datasets_category
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ # Find all vaccination datasets cdc_datasets_category("Vaccination")  # Find COVID-19 related datasets cdc_datasets_category("COVID-19")  # Search within a category cdc_datasets_category("Vaccination", query = "influenza")  # Exact category match cdc_datasets_category("NCHS", exact = TRUE) } # }

title: Search Datasets by Tag — cdc_datasets_tag
text: Finds datasets specific tag data.cdc.gov. Use cdc_tags() see available tags.

title: Search Datasets by Tag — cdc_datasets_tag
code: cdc_datasets_tag(tag, query = NULL, limit = 20, exact = FALSE)

title: Search Datasets by Tag — cdc_datasets_tag
text: tag Character. tag name search . Supports partial matching (case-insensitive) default. query Character. Optional additional search terms filter results. limit Integer. Maximum number results (default 20, max 100). exact Logical. TRUE, requires exact tag match. FALSE (default), uses partial matching.

title: Search Datasets by Tag — cdc_datasets_tag
text: data.frame columns: id: Dataset identifier use cdc_query() name: Dataset title description: Brief description category: Dataset category tags: Comma-separated list tags updated_at: Last update timestamp rows: Approximate row count (available) url: Direct link dataset page

title: Search Datasets by Tag — cdc_datasets_tag
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ # Find datasets tagged with "covid-19" cdc_datasets_tag("covid-19")  # Find mortality-related datasets cdc_datasets_tag("mortality")  # Search within tagged datasets cdc_datasets_tag("vaccination", query = "children")  # Exact tag match cdc_datasets_tag("brfss", exact = TRUE) } # }

what: Get Distinct Values for a Column — cdc_distinct
title: Get Distinct Values for a Column — cdc_distinct
text: Returns unique values column dataset. Useful exploring categorical variables.

title: Get Distinct Values for a Column — cdc_distinct
code: cdc_distinct(dataset_id, column, where = NULL, limit = 100, ...)

title: Get Distinct Values for a Column — cdc_distinct
text: dataset_id Character. unique dataset identifier (e.g., "swc5-untb" PLACES data). can find dataset URL via cdc_datasets(). column Character. column name. Character. SoQL clause filtering rows. Example: "year = '2023' stateabbr = 'MN'" limit Integer. Maximum distinct values return (default 100). ... Arguments passed cdc_query()

title: Get Distinct Values for a Column — cdc_distinct
text: data.frame distinct values counts.

title: Get Distinct Values for a Column — cdc_distinct
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ cdc_distinct("swc5-untb", "stateabbr")  cdc_distinct("swc5-untb", "year") } # }

what: Fetch Large CDC Datasets with Pagination — cdc_fetch
title: Fetch Large CDC Datasets with Pagination — cdc_fetch
text: Retrieves large datasets automatically paginating results. Shows progress bar long-running queries.

title: Fetch Large CDC Datasets with Pagination — cdc_fetch
code: cdc_fetch(   dataset_id,   select = NULL,   where = NULL,   order = NULL,   q = NULL,   max_rows = Inf,   page_size = 1000,   progress = interactive(),   as_csv = FALSE )

title: Fetch Large CDC Datasets with Pagination — cdc_fetch
text: dataset_id Character. unique dataset identifier (e.g., "swc5-untb" PLACES data). can find dataset URL via cdc_datasets(). select Character vector. Columns return. Use NULL columns. Character. SoQL clause filtering rows. Example: "year = '2023' stateabbr = 'MN'" order Character. Column(s) sort . Use "column DESC" descending. q Character. Full-text search query across text fields. max_rows Integer. Maximum total rows fetch. Use Inf rows. page_size Integer. Rows per API request (default 1000, max 50000). progress Logical. Show progress bar? Default TRUE interactive sessions. as_csv Logical. TRUE, requests data CSV format instead JSON. CSV can faster large datasets may handle data types differently. Default FALSE.

title: Fetch Large CDC Datasets with Pagination — cdc_fetch
text: data.frame containing fetched rows.

title: Fetch Large CDC Datasets with Pagination — cdc_fetch
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ places_mn <- cdc_fetch(   "swc5-untb",   where = "stateabbr = 'MN'",   max_rows = 5000 )  all_data <- cdc_fetch("swc5-untb", where = "year = '2023'")  # Use CSV format for faster downloads cdc_fetch("swc5-untb", where = "stateabbr = 'CA'", as_csv = TRUE) } # }

title: Get Dataset Metadata — cdc_metadata
text: Retrieves detailed metadata specific CDC dataset, including column names, types, descriptions, dataset-level information.

title: Get Dataset Metadata — cdc_metadata
code: cdc_metadata(   dataset_id,   include_columns = TRUE,   include_rowcount = TRUE,   cleaned = TRUE,   progress = interactive() )

title: Get Dataset Metadata — cdc_metadata
text: dataset_id Character. dataset identifier. include_columns Logical. TRUE (default), includes column-level metadata. include_rowcount Logical. TRUE (default), creates row count metadata. Row count available api metadata created manually using cdc_count(). cleaned Logical. TRUE (default), returns cleaned metadata. FALSE, provides raw metadata including row_count (unless include_rowcount = FALSE). progress Logical. TRUE (default), shows function progress outputs console.

title: Get Dataset Metadata — cdc_metadata
text: list containing: id: Dataset identifier name: Dataset title description: Full description attribution: Data source attribution category: Dataset category tags: Associated tags created_at: Creation timestamp updated_at: Last update timestamp row_count: Row count (include_rowcount = TRUE) columns: data.frame column metadata (include_columns = TRUE)

title: Get Dataset Metadata — cdc_metadata
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ cdc_metadata("swc5-untb") } # }

title: Preview Dataset Sample — cdc_preview
text: Returns small sample rows dataset quick inspection.

title: Preview Dataset Sample — cdc_preview
code: cdc_preview(dataset_id, n = 10, ...)

title: Preview Dataset Sample — cdc_preview
text: dataset_id Character. unique dataset identifier (e.g., "swc5-untb" PLACES data). can find dataset URL via cdc_datasets(). n Integer. Number rows return (default 10). ... Arguments passed cdc_query()

title: Preview Dataset Sample — cdc_preview
text: data.frame sample rows.

title: Preview Dataset Sample — cdc_preview
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ cdc_preview("swc5-untb") } # }

title: Query a CDC Dataset — cdc_query
text: Fetches data CDC dataset data.cdc.gov using Socrata API. Supports flexible querying via SoQL (Socrata Query Language).

title: Query a CDC Dataset — cdc_query
code: cdc_query(   dataset_id,   select = NULL,   where = NULL,   order = NULL,   group = NULL,   having = NULL,   q = NULL,   limit = 1000,   offset = NULL,   as = c("dataframe", "list", "raw"),   as_csv = FALSE,   progress = interactive(),   quiet_token = FALSE )

title: Query a CDC Dataset — cdc_query
text: dataset_id Character. unique dataset identifier (e.g., "swc5-untb" PLACES data). can find dataset URL via cdc_datasets(). select Character vector. Columns return. Use NULL columns. Character. SoQL clause filtering rows. Example: "year = '2023' stateabbr = 'MN'" order Character. Column(s) sort . Use "column DESC" descending. group Character. Column(s) group aggregation. Character. SoQL clause filtering aggregated results. q Character. Full-text search query across text fields. limit Integer. Maximum rows return. Default 1000, max 50000 per request. offset Integer. Number rows skip (manual pagination). Character. Output format: "dataframe" (default), "list", "raw". as_csv Logical. TRUE, requests data CSV format instead JSON. CSV can faster large datasets may handle data types differently. Default FALSE. progress Logical. TRUE, shows progress messages console. quiet_token Logical. TRUE, show console alerts cdc_app_token().

title: Query a CDC Dataset — cdc_query
text: data.frame (default), list, raw string depending .

title: Query a CDC Dataset — cdc_query
text: Common clause operators: Comparison: =, !=, <, >, <=, >= Logical: , , Text: LIKE '%pattern%', starts_with(), contains() Null checks: NULL, NULL Lists: ('', 'b', 'c') Aggregation functions (use group): count(*), sum(), avg(), min(), max()

title: Query a CDC Dataset — cdc_query
text: JSON (default) preserves data types handles nested structures. CSV may faster large flat datasets converts everything strings.

title: Query a CDC Dataset — cdc_query
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ cdc_query("swc5-untb", limit = 100)  cdc_query(   "swc5-untb",   select = c("stateabbr", "locationname", "access2_crudeprev"),   where = "stateabbr = 'MN' AND year = '2023'",   order = "access2_crudeprev DESC",   limit = 50 )  cdc_query(   "swc5-untb",   select = c("stateabbr", "count(*) as n"),   group = "stateabbr",   order = "n DESC" )  # Use CSV format for potentially faster downloads cdc_query("swc5-untb", limit = 100, as_csv = TRUE) } # }

title: List Available Tags — cdc_tags
text: Returns list dataset tags available data.cdc.gov. Use tag names cdc_datasets_tag().

title: List Available Tags — cdc_tags
code: cdc_tags(limit = 100)

title: List Available Tags — cdc_tags
text: limit Integer. Maximum number datasets sample extracting tags (default 100). Higher values give complete tag lists slower.

title: List Available Tags — cdc_tags
text: data.frame columns: tag: Tag name count: Number datasets tag

title: List Available Tags — cdc_tags
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ # List all tags cdc_tags()  # See which tags are most common cdc_tags() |> dplyr::arrange(dplyr::desc(count)) } # }

what: Perform API request and parse response — perform_request
title: Perform API request and parse response — perform_request
text: Perform API request parse response

title: Perform API request and parse response — perform_request
code: perform_request(req, as = "dataframe", format = "json")

title: Perform API request and parse response — perform_request
text: req httr2 request object. Character. Output format: "dataframe", "list", "raw". format Character. Response format: "json" "csv".

title: Perform API request and parse response — perform_request
text: Parsed response requested format.
